title: Opening Our Tiny Ramen Shop on a Quiet Lane
title_zh: 在僻靜巷弄開起小拉麵店

all.en: My husband and I had dreamed of opening a ramen shop for almost ten years.
all.zh: 我和先生夢想開一家拉麵店已經快十年了。

all.en: We finally found a small space on a quiet lane behind the train station.
all.zh: 我們終於在火車站後面的一條安靜小巷裡找到了一個小店面。

all.en: The rent was affordable, and the kitchen had just enough room for two cooks.
all.zh: 租金不貴，廚房剛好容得下兩位廚師工作。

all.en: We spent three months scrubbing the walls, painting the ceiling, and replacing old tiles.
all.zh: 我們花了三個月刷牆、漆天花板，並更換老舊的磁磚。

all.en: My husband perfected the pork broth, simmering bones for eighteen hours every test batch.
all.zh: 我先生致力於完善豬骨湯頭，每一次試做都熬煮十八個小時。

all.en: I focused on the noodles, learning to mix flour and water by feel alone.
all.zh: 我則專注於麵條，學著單憑手感調配麵粉和水的比例。

all.en: Our neighbors often poked their heads in to ask when we would finally open.
all.zh: 鄰居們常常探頭進來，問我們究竟什麼時候才要正式開幕。

all.en: We chose a soft opening day in late October, hoping for cool weather and steady appetites.
all.zh: 我們選在十月底試營運，希望天氣涼爽，客人胃口也好。

all.en: On the morning of the opening, my hands trembled as I tied my apron strings.
all.zh: 開幕當天早上，我綁圍裙繩帶時雙手都在發抖。

all.en: By eleven o'clock, a short line had already formed outside our wooden sliding door.
all.zh: 十一點時，我們的木製拉門外已經排起了一小列隊伍。

all.en: Our first customer was an elderly man who said he loved the smell of pork broth.
all.zh: 我們的第一位客人是位老先生，他說他很喜歡豬骨湯頭的香氣。

all.en: He ordered a basic shoyu ramen and slurped every drop of soup from the bowl.
all.zh: 他點了一碗基本款的醬油拉麵，連最後一滴湯都喝得乾乾淨淨。

all.en: When he gave us a quiet thumbs up, my husband nearly cried into his ladle.
all.zh: 當他靜靜地比出大拇指時，我先生差點對著湯杓掉眼淚。

all.en: Word spread quickly through the neighborhood, and lunch hours grew busier each day.
all.zh: 消息在街坊間迅速傳開，午餐時段一天比一天忙碌。

all.en: We learned to move around the tiny kitchen without bumping into each other.
all.zh: 我們學會在狹小的廚房裡來回穿梭，不會撞到對方。

all.en: Some days we ran out of broth by two in the afternoon and had to close early.
all.zh: 有些日子我們的湯頭下午兩點就賣完了，只好提早打烊。

all.en: Regular customers started bringing their own friends, parents, and visiting relatives.
all.zh: 常客開始帶他們的朋友、父母和來訪的親戚一起上門。

all.en: A college student told us our ramen reminded her of meals she once had in Tokyo.
all.zh: 一位大學生告訴我們，我們的拉麵讓她想起以前在東京吃過的味道。

all.en: We added a spicy miso option after several customers requested something with more kick.
all.zh: 在幾位客人要求口味更刺激後，我們新增了辣味噌口味。

all.en: My husband still tweaked the broth every week, never quite satisfied with the depth.
all.zh: 我先生每週仍會微調湯頭，總覺得深度還不夠完美。

all.en: I started arriving at five in the morning to roll noodles for the busy lunch crowd.
all.zh: 我開始早上五點就到店裡，為中午的人潮擀好麵條。

all.en: We hired one part-time helper, a friendly student who washed dishes between classes.
all.zh: 我們聘了一位兼職幫手，是位友善的學生，下課之間來幫忙洗碗。

all.en: On rainy days, the warmth of the shop seemed to pull people in from the street.
all.zh: 下雨天時，店裡的暖意彷彿把街上的人們吸引進來。

all.en: A local food blogger visited one afternoon and posted a glowing review the next morning.
all.zh: 一位在地美食部落客某天下午來訪，隔天早上就發表了一篇極好的評論。

all.en: Suddenly our quiet lane was full of strangers waiting patiently for a small bowl of noodles.
all.zh: 突然間，我們安靜的小巷擠滿了陌生人，耐心等候著一碗小小的麵。

all.en: We felt grateful, but also a little overwhelmed by the sudden attention.
all.zh: 我們心懷感激，但這突如其來的關注也讓我們有點不知所措。

all.en: We decided to keep the menu small and refused to expand into a second location.
all.zh: 我們決定維持簡單的菜單，並拒絕擴張開設第二家店面。

all.en: Quality mattered more to us than rapid growth or impressive sales numbers.
all.zh: 對我們來說，品質遠比快速擴張或亮眼的營業額重要。

all.en: Our elderly first customer became a weekly regular, sitting at the same corner stool.
all.zh: 我們的第一位老先生客人變成了每週常客，總坐在同一個角落的椅子上。

all.en: He once told us that our shop felt like a small piece of home he had been missing.
all.zh: 他曾告訴我們，我們的店感覺就像他一直在尋找的、家的一小片角落。

all.en: On our one-year anniversary, we offered free soft-boiled eggs to every guest.
all.zh: 在我們開店一週年那天，我們招待每位客人一顆免費的溏心蛋。

all.en: The line stretched down the lane and around the corner, far longer than ever before.
all.zh: 隊伍從小巷延伸到轉角，比以往任何時候都還要長。

all.en: Neighbors brought flowers, and one even hung a hand-painted banner above our door.
all.zh: 鄰居們送來鮮花，其中一位還在我們門上掛了一幅手繪的橫幅。

all.en: That night, after closing, we sat quietly in the empty shop and shared one final bowl.
all.zh: 那晚打烊後，我們靜靜地坐在空蕩的店裡，分享最後一碗麵。

all.en: We knew then that this tiny ramen shop had become much more than just our dream.
all.zh: 那一刻我們明白，這家小小的拉麵店已不僅僅是我們的夢想。

all.en: In the second year, we began closing every Wednesday to rest and develop new recipes.
all.zh: 進入第二年後，我們開始每週三公休，用來休息並研發新菜單。

all.en: My husband traveled south to learn from an old master who specialized in tonkotsu broth.
all.zh: 我先生南下向一位專精於豚骨湯頭的老師傅學習。

all.en: He returned with new techniques and a notebook full of carefully drawn timing charts.
all.zh: 他帶著新技巧和一本仔細繪製火候圖的筆記本回來了。

all.en: We invited our regulars to taste-test the new broth before adding it to the menu.
all.zh: 我們邀請熟客先試喝新湯頭，再決定是否加入菜單。

all.en: The student helper graduated and surprised us by asking to work full time after school.
all.zh: 學生幫手畢業後讓我們驚喜，他主動提出畢業後想轉成全職員工。

all.en: We agreed gladly and taught him how to read the broth's surface for perfect timing.
all.zh: 我們欣然同意，並教他如何觀察湯面來掌握最佳火候。

all.en: On cold winter evenings, the windows fogged up with steam from the simmering pots.
all.zh: 寒冷的冬夜裡，店內的窗戶因熬煮湯鍋蒸騰的熱氣而起霧。

all.en: A young couple even asked to hold a small proposal dinner inside our shop one Sunday.
all.zh: 一對年輕情侶甚至要求某個星期天在我們店裡舉行小型求婚晚餐。

all.en: We closed early that day, hung warm lanterns, and prepared a special private set menu.
all.zh: 那天我們提早打烊，掛上溫暖的燈籠，並準備了特別的私房套餐。

all.en: Watching them say yes among rising steam reminded us why we built this small dream at all.
all.zh: 看著他們在裊裊蒸氣中互許承諾，讓我們再次明白當初為何要打造這個小小夢想。

all.en: The young couple returned for their wedding anniversary every spring after that memorable proposal night.
all.zh: 那對年輕情侶在難忘的求婚夜後，每年春天都回來慶祝結婚週年。

all.en: We learned their favorite orders and reserved their corner table without ever needing a phone call.
all.zh: 我們記住了他們最喜歡的餐點，連電話都不必打就為他們保留角落那張桌子。

all.en: Other regulars began celebrating birthdays, promotions, and small reunions inside our tiny ramen shop.
all.zh: 其他常客也開始在我們小小的拉麵店裡慶祝生日、升遷與小型聚會。

all.en: My husband added a quiet rule, no large cake candles indoors, but still welcomed every gathering warmly.
all.zh: 我先生加了一條低調的規定：店內不放大型蛋糕蠟燭，但仍熱情歡迎每一場聚會。

all.en: During the third year, a local culinary school asked if students could intern in our kitchen.
all.zh: 進入第三年時，一所在地餐飲學校詢問是否能讓學生來我們廚房實習。

all.en: We agreed to take two students per semester and taught them everything we had patiently learned.
all.zh: 我們同意每學期收兩位學生，把我們耐心學會的一切毫無保留地教給他們。

all.en: One quiet student named Aki later opened his own small noodle bar in his hometown by the sea.
all.zh: 一位安靜的學生名叫 Aki，後來在他海邊的家鄉開了自己的一家小型麵店。

all.en: He mailed us photos of his opening day with a heartfelt note thanking us for our example.
all.zh: 他寄來開幕當天的照片，附上一張誠摯的字條感謝我們作為他的榜樣。

all.en: On rainy autumn evenings, I sometimes still cry quietly while wiping down the wooden counters.
all.zh: 在下雨的秋日傍晚，我有時還是會一邊擦拭木製吧台一邊靜靜地落淚。

all.en: Our tiny shop had grown bigger than us, and it had truly become a small piece of home.
all.zh: 我們的小店已經成長得超越了我們本身，並真正成為一小片屬於大家的家。

all.en: We added a small handwritten chalkboard outside to thank each regular by their first name.
all.zh: 我們在門外加了一塊小黑板，用名字親手寫下感謝每位常客。

all.en: Our part-time helper, now full-time, suggested we start delivering ramen to elderly customers nearby.
all.zh: 我們原本的兼職幫手如今轉成全職，建議我們開始為附近的長者顧客外送拉麵。

all.en: Every Tuesday afternoon, he packed warm bowls into insulated bags and rode his bicycle around the neighborhood.
all.zh: 每個週二下午，他把熱騰騰的麵裝進保溫袋，騎著腳踏車繞著社區送餐。

all.en: An elderly widow living three lanes away cried the first time he arrived at her front door.
all.zh: 住在三條巷子外的一位獨居老太太，在他第一次出現在門口時感動落淚。

all.en: We started keeping a small notebook of customers' favorite toppings and special dietary needs by hand.
all.zh: 我們開始用手抄一本小筆記，記下每位客人最愛的配料和特殊飲食需求。

all.en: On rainy days, my husband would sneak an extra slice of pork into bowls for tired-looking visitors.
all.zh: 下雨天時，我先生會偷偷在看起來疲憊的客人碗裡多加一片豬肉。

all.en: A film student spent a quiet afternoon documenting our broth-making process for her graduation project.
all.zh: 一位電影系學生花了一個安靜的下午，記錄我們熬湯的過程作為她的畢業作品。

all.en: Her short film won a small festival prize and we hung the printed certificate near the kitchen.
all.zh: 她的短片在一個小型影展獲獎，我們把列印出的獎狀掛在廚房旁邊。

all.en: We turned down two franchise offers because keeping things small was more important than the money.
all.zh: 我們婉拒了兩個加盟邀約，因為保持小規模對我們而言比金錢更重要。

all.en: Some nights, I still stand at the counter and quietly thank the wooden door for every soul it has welcomed.
all.zh: 有些夜晚，我仍會站在吧台前，靜靜地感謝那扇木門所迎進的每一個靈魂。

all.en: My husband and I dreamed of opening a ramen shop for nearly ten years.
all.zh: 我和先生夢想開一間拉麵店，夢了將近十年。

all.en: We finally found a small space on a quiet lane behind the station.
all.zh: 我們終於在車站後一條僻靜巷弄裡，找到一間小店面。

all.en: The rent was affordable, and the kitchen just fit the two of us.
all.zh: 租金負擔得起，廚房剛好容得下我們兩人。

all.en: We tasted dozens of broth recipes before we settled on the right one.
all.zh: 我們試了幾十種高湯配方，才定下最對味的那一個。

all.en: Please come back when we open; the first bowl is on the house.
all.zh: 我們開幕時請回來，第一碗我們請客。

all.en: On opening day, neighbors brought flowers and hung a banner above the door.
all.zh: 開幕當天，鄰居們送花，還在門上掛了塊布條。

all.en: We were so nervous that we kept dropping chopsticks all morning.
all.zh: 我們緊張到整個早上一直把筷子掉到地上。

all.en: Running your own shop is exhausting, but every smile makes it worth it.
all.zh: 開自己的店很累，但每個笑容都讓一切值得。

all.en: Regulars now have their own favorite seats by the wooden window.
all.zh: 常客現在都有自己最愛的木窗邊座位了。

all.en: Some nights I still thank that little door for everyone it welcomes in.
all.zh: 有些夜裡，我仍會感謝那扇小門迎進的每一個人。
